title: Physical and Logical Security, Identity, Authentication, and Access · Support Decision Defense
summary: A support panel compares near-miss cases for physical and logical security, identity, authentication, and access and accepts only safe, authorized, causal, accessible, privacy-preserving, and independently defensible decisions. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for identity and least-privilege access model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Classify physical, technical, administrative, preventive, deterrent, detective, corrective, compensating, and directive controls by threat and evidence.
Separate person, device, workload, account, identifier, credential, authenticator, authentication, authorization, session, role, group, and audit event.
Compare knowledge, possession, inherence, location, behavior, certificates, tokens, biometrics, MFA, passwordless, fallback, recovery, and enrollment risks.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Physical Access
steps.instruction:
quiz case — People, accounts, credentials, devices, roles, badges, sessions, groups, guests, and service identities are conflated after unauthorized access. The quiz variation 4 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Layer doors, locks, badges, guards, cameras, lighting, visitor controls, equipment locks, privacy screens, secure areas, tailgating resistance, and emergency access.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Physical Access.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for layer doors, locks, badges, guards, cameras, lighting, visitor controls, equipment locks, privacy screens, secure areas, tailgating resistance, and emergency access. For CompTIA A+ support Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Physical Access, keep Physical Access connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: People, accounts, credentials, devices, roles, badges, sessions, groups, guests, and service identities are conflated after unauthorized access. The quiz variation 4 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Layer doors, locks, badges, guards, cameras, lighting, visitor controls, equipment locks, privacy screens, secure areas, tailgating resistance, and emergency access.

content.title: Fault-isolation decision
content.text: Select evidence that identifies a cause, not a patch that only hides the Physical Access symptom.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: CompTIA A+ Core 1 220-1201 Exam Objectives — Controls every current Core 1 mobile, networking, hardware, virtualization, cloud, and troubleshooting objective and weighting.

stimulus.title: Physical Access failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A locked server-room door protects equipment from every physical and environmental threat.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Layer doors, locks, badges, guards, cameras, lighting, visitor controls, equipment locks, privacy screens, secure areas, tailgating resistance, and emergency access.

options.text: Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority. That claim fails the evidence boundary for Physical Access.

For diagnose the hidden failure, apply one familiar repair, omit user impact, authorization, safety, asset and version identity, baseline, recent change, competing cause, prediction, observation, rollback, changed-case verification, accessibility, privacy, escalation, and controlled transfer, then accept a missing symptom as support proof.
Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Physical Access
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Physical Access: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — People, accounts, credentials, devices, roles, badges, sessions, groups, guests, and service identities are conflated after unauthorized access. The quiz variation 6 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Layer doors, locks, badges, guards, cameras, lighting, visitor controls, equipment locks, privacy screens, secure areas, tailgating resistance, and emergency access.

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Physical Access.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For CompTIA A+ support Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Physical Access, keep Physical Access connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Layer doors, locks, badges, guards, cameras, lighting, visitor controls, equipment locks, privacy screens, secure areas, tailgating resistance, and emergency access.

hints: For CompTIA A+ support Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Physical Access, name the user impact, fictional ticket, asset, environment, authorization, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, prevention, escalation, accessible documentation, and transfer boundary, Physical Access precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At CompTIA A+ support Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Physical Access, trace this concrete input and predict user impact, safety and authority, baseline, reproduction, timeline, hypothesis, bounded test, observation, rejection, repair, rollback, original and changed-case verification, prevention, escalation, and controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: People, accounts, credentials, devices, roles, badges, sessions, groups, guests, and service identities are conflated after unauthorized access. The quiz variation 6 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Layer doors, locks, badges, guards, cameras, lighting, visitor controls, equipment locks, privacy screens, secure areas, tailgating resistance, and emergency access.

For CompTIA A+ support Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Physical Access, challenge the documented misconception with changed-user, device, version, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, access need, hypothesis, test result, repair, restart, recurrence, escalation, and controlled equipment-transfer evidence: A locked server-room door protects equipment from every physical and environmental threat.

checks.description: The decision applies Physical Access to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Physical Access.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using layer and doors plus specific case evidence.
